topic: interpreting data sets, counting data values, identifying units
latex:
You keep track of the number of pages you read each day for one week. Your results are:

$45, 30, 52, 38, 41$

\textbf{Part A} Find and interpret the number of data values.

There are \underline{\hspace{1cm}} data values. So, you tracked reading for \underline{\hspace{1cm}} days.

\textbf{Part B} What are the units?

\begin{enumerate}
\item[a.] $\bigcirc$ miles
\item[b.] $\bigcirc$ pages
\item[c.] $\bigcirc$ minutes
\item[d.] $\bigcirc$ kilograms
\end{enumerate}
answer_latex: Part A: 5 data values, 5 days; Part B: b. pages
